bullets.text: Satellite images analyzed by BBC Verify show that over 1,400 buildings in Lebanese villages have been destroyed since March 2, revealing the scale of Israeli demolitions.
South African opposition figure Julius Malema has been sentenced to five years in prison for a gun offence, with Malema appealing the decision.
Donors have pledged over £1 billion for Sudan at a conference in Berlin, exceeding the funding target amidst a deepening humanitarian crisis.
Chef José Andrés, founder of World Central Kitchen, has warned of a potential multi-year world famine resulting from the Iran war, predicting a 'silent' collapse of the global fertilizer trade.
The UK government is developing contingency plans to address potential supermarket shelf gaps by summer, anticipating disruptions to CO2 supply if the Iran war persists.
Defence Minister Richard Marles stated that the US alliance remains indispensable for Australian national security, even as Australia aims to build a more self-reliant defence force.
Lebanese officials anticipate an imminent ceasefire with Israel, which could support broader efforts for a US-Iran peace accord.
Pakistan is mediating to extend the US-Iran ceasefire beyond its upcoming expiry, aiming for more time to negotiate a lasting peace deal.
Gulf states are increasingly using private deals for fundraising, with over $10 billion raised, as the conflict with Iran impacts their economies.
